title: King Richard
logline: Richard Williams, a determined father from Compton, fights against all odds to train his daughters Venus and Serena to become tennis champions, navigating poverty, racism, and the elite tennis world to protect their childhood while pursuing their dreams.
antagonistOrConflict: Systemic racism, poverty, and the elite tennis establishment that seeks to exploit young talent
tone: Inspirational yet gritty, balancing hope with harsh realities

actBreaks.description: Paul Cohen agrees to coach Venus, marking the family's first breakthrough into the professional tennis world.

validation.ruling: Richard and his daughters arrive at the elite Agoura Springs Hotel where they encounter John McEnroe and Pete Sampras practicing with coach Paul Cohen, marking their irreversible entry into the professional tennis world and fundamentally changing their trajectory from Compton courts to elite coaching.

actBreaks.description: Three years later at Macci's academy, Venus has transformed into a formidable player ready for professional competition.

validation.ruling: Richard and Venus have their emotional confrontation on the tennis court where she demonstrates her incredible power and skill, leading to his decision to finally let her turn professional - the point of no return that launches her into the professional arena.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script follows a classic underdog journey but distinguishes itself through Richard's protective parenting philosophy. Rather than rushing toward success, the story emphasizes his controversial decision to limit tournaments and preserve his daughters' childhoods. The structure builds methodically from rejection to acceptance, with each act representing a new level of the tennis world - from local courts to elite coaching to professional competition. The pacing deliberately mirrors Richard's patient approach, taking time to develop character relationships and family dynamics rather than rushing toward tennis victories.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The first act moves quickly through multiple rejections (scenes 4-11) before slowing down to establish family life in Compton. The middle section accelerates through a tournament montage (scenes 43-50) then decelerates for character development at Macci's academy. The final act builds tension steadily toward Venus's professional debut, with the climactic match spanning multiple scenes (92-101).

incitingIncident.description: Vic Braden watches Richard's homemade video of Venus and Serena playing tennis while Richard narrates and pitches their abilities.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. L.A. COUNTRY CLUB - VIC BRADEN’S OFFICE - DAY

validation.ruling: Richard shows his homemade video to tennis coach Vic Braden, marking the first time he actively pursues professional coaching for his daughters, shattering their isolated training world and establishing the main conflict of gaining acceptance in elite tennis.

midpoint.description: Richard tells Paul he's pulling Venus out of junior tournaments and refusing to sign with agents, shocking both Paul and Oracene with his sudden decision.

validation.ruling: Richard makes the shocking decision to pull Venus out of junior tournaments and refuse to sign with agents, fundamentally shifting from reactive responses to outside pressure to proactively controlling his daughters' path, despite opposition from Paul and Oracene.

climax.description: Venus battles through early nerves with double faults and errors while her family and Nike representatives watch anxiously, as Richard paces nervously encouraging her.
climax.sceneHeading: EXT. SATELLITE COURT - OAKLAND COLOSSEUM ARENA - DAY

validation.ruling: Venus battles through her professional debut match with early nerves, double faults and errors while her family watches anxiously - this is the peak confrontation where she must prove herself against a seasoned professional opponent, demonstrating her readiness for the elite level.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEVERLY HILLS, CALIFORNIA - 1989 (ESTABLISHING)
scenes.summary: Establishing shots of Beverly Hills mansions, palm trees, and luxury cars.

scenes.heading: EXT. PALOS VERDES COUNTRY CLUB - VARIOUS SHOTS - MORNING
scenes.summary: Exterior shots of the majestic Palos Verdes Country Club with pristine tennis courts and wealthy white people.

scenes.heading: INT. PALOS VERDES COUNTRY CLUB - MORNING
scenes.summary: Richard Williams stands in the pro shop receiving discarded tennis balls from a tennis pro, saying they're not dead to his family.

scenes.heading: EXT. PALOS VERDES COUNTRY CLUB - VARIOUS SHOTS - MORNING
scenes.summary: Richard walks the country club grounds collecting stray balls from trash while narrating his approach to learning tennis.

scenes.heading: INT. RICHARD’S VW BUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Richard drives his VW bus filled with tennis equipment and self-help materials while listening to tennis instruction tapes.

scenes.heading: EXT. FANCY TENNIS CLUB - DAY
scenes.summary: Richard gives a sales pitch to an uninterested tennis pro at a fancy club, handing him a homemade brochure.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANOTHER FANCY TENNIS CLUB - DAY
scenes.summary: Richard continues his presentation to Bud Collins poolside, explaining his 78-page plan for his daughters' careers.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANOTHER FANCY TENNIS CLUB - DAY
scenes.summary: Richard tells a dismissive pro about watching Virginia Ruzici win $40,000 and deciding to have two more kids for tennis.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANOTHER FANCY TENNIS CLUB - DAY
scenes.summary: Richard explains to another baffled pro that his plan covers Venus and Serena's tennis, education, and languages.

scenes.heading: EXT. COUNTRY CLUB - POOLSIDE - DAY
scenes.summary: Bud Collins examines Richard's homemade pamphlet filled with numbers, graphs, and future earnings projections.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANOTHER FANCY TENNIS CLUB - DAY
scenes.summary: Richard tells the baffled pro on court that they're not there to rob him but to make him rich.

scenes.heading: EXT. FANCY TENNIS CLUB - DAY
scenes.summary: The uninterested pro hands Richard's brochure back and suggests he consider basketball instead.

scenes.heading: EXT. COUNTRY CLUB - POOLSIDE - DAY
scenes.summary: Bud Collins returns the brochure wishing Richard luck, who responds that Bud's making a mistake.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANOTHER FANCY TENNIS CLUB - DAY
scenes.summary: The baffled pro returns Richard's pamphlet saying he'd be rich if he had a dollar for every crazy parent claiming their kid will be number one.

scenes.heading: EXT. COMPTON, CALIFORNIA - VARIOUS SHOTS - DAY
scenes.summary: Richard's bus drives through Compton, showing a diverse working-class neighborhood with gangs, drug wars, and street life.

scenes.heading: EXT. WILLIAMS’ HOME (COMPTON) - DAY
scenes.summary: Eight-year-old Serena and nine-year-old Venus Williams carry phone books from their front lawn, racing and competing with each other.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. RICHARD’S VW BUS (MOVING) - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Richard drives his five daughters in the VW bus while they do homework and joke, discussing where they'll practice tennis.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST COMPTON PARK - DAY (VARIOUS SHOTS)
scenes.summary: The Williams family arrives at East Compton Park's deteriorated tennis courts marked with gunfire and littered with drug paraphernalia.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST COMPTON PARK - TENNIS COURTS - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: After practice, gang member Roc confronts Richard about talking to 16-year-old Tunde, escalating to physical confrontation.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. RICHARD'S VW VAN - DUSK
scenes.summary: Richard drives home with a swollen face after being beaten, telling his daughters about fighting daily as a child.

scenes.heading: EXT. WILLIAMS’ HOME (COMPTON) - NIGHT (LATER)
scenes.summary: The family arrives home to see police cruiser lights and a neighbor threatening to call cops on them.

scenes.heading: INT. WILLIAMS’ HOME (COMPTON) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The five daughters enter their small home where Oracene prepares dinner and they plan a family talent show.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE (COMPTON) - GIRLS’ BEDROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Richard puts the girls to bed in their cramped bedroom, reminding them about planning and not failing.

scenes.heading: EXT. SWAP MEET (COMPTON) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Richard's bus arrives at a bleak Compton strip mall outside a swap meet late at night.

scenes.heading: INT. SWAP MEET (COMPTON) - AISLES/OFFICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Richard unlocks his desk drawer, removes a pistol to holster on his belt, then works on tennis materials and his 78-page plan.

scenes.heading: INT. L.A. COUNTRY CLUB - VIC BRADEN’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Vic Braden watches Richard's homemade video of Venus and Serena playing tennis while Richard narrates and pitches their abilities.
scenes.turningPointNote: First major breakthrough - Richard successfully gets a respected tennis coach to watch his daughters' tape

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST COMPTON PARK - TENNIS COURTS - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Venus and Serena practice under streetlights on the deteriorated courts while Richard looks wrung out and says nothing.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. FAST FOOD RESTAURANT - MAGIC HOUR
scenes.summary: The Williams family prays before their fast food meal while Richard sits distracted and distant from the conversation.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST COMPTON PARK - TENNIS COURTS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Richard practices alone on the dark courts when Roc and gang members arrive looking for Tunde, leading to another confrontation.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. RICHARD'S VW BUS (MOVING) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Richard drives through Compton bleeding and armed, hunting for the gang members who beat him up.

scenes.heading: EXT. D&K DONUTS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Richard waits across from D&K Donuts with his gun as a drive-by shooting erupts, shattering the shop window.

scenes.heading: INT. WILLIAMS’ HOME (COMPTON) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Richard returns home shaken and bloodied where Oracene tends to his wounds and they discuss their situation.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. RICHARD'S VW BUS (BEVERLY HILLS) - DAY
scenes.summary: Richard drives Venus and Serena through Beverly Hills, showing them mansions and asking which houses they like.

scenes.heading: EXT. AGOURA SPRINGS HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Richard's van rolls through the gates of the Agoura Springs Hotel.

scenes.heading: EXT. AGOURA SPRINGS HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Richard and the girls watch John McEnroe and Pete Sampras practice before meeting coach Paul Cohen, who agrees to train Venus.
scenes.turningPointNote: Major breakthrough - Paul Cohen agrees to coach Venus, but only Venus, creating family tension

scenes.heading: INT. WILLIAMS’ HOUSE (COMPTON) - DAY (A FEW HOURS LATER)
scenes.summary: The family celebrates Venus getting a coach, but learns Paul will only train Venus for free, not both sisters.

scenes.heading: EXT. WILLIAMS’ HOME (COMPTON) - MORNING
scenes.summary: Serena watches sadly as Richard and Venus load tennis gear, expressing that it's not fair she can't go.

scenes.heading: INT. WILLIAMS' HOME (COMPTON) - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Oracene tells Serena she's not left out and that she has something great too - her mother as coach.

scenes.heading: EXT. AGOURA SPRINGS HOTEL - TENNIS COURT - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul leads Venus through grueling sprint workouts while explaining his strategic tennis system and Richard films with a camcorder.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST COMPTON PARK - TENNIS COURTS - DAY
scenes.summary: Serena runs ball-less drills on the Compton courts while Oracene shouts encouragement for her to be faster.

scenes.heading: EXT. AGOURA SPRINGS HOTEL - TENNIS COURT - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul hits cross-court with Venus while Richard interferes with coaching advice, creating tension between the two men.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST COMPTON PARK - TENNIS COURTS - DAY
scenes.summary: Serena practices serve tosses with Oracene's encouragement until she nails it and they high-five.

scenes.heading: EXT. AGOURA SPRINGS HOTEL - TENNIS COURT - DAY
scenes.summary: Richard films Venus working with Paul on serves while continuing to interfere with coaching advice, frustrating Paul.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST COMPTON PARK - TENNIS COURTS - DAY
scenes.summary: Oracene coaches Serena through another tough workout, pumping her up throughout the session.

scenes.heading: EXT. AGOURA SPRINGS HOTEL - TENNIS COURT - DAY
scenes.summary: Richard steps onto the court to interrupt Paul's practice with Venus, creating a heated confrontation about coaching methods.

scenes.heading: EXT. AGOURA SPRINGS HOTEL - PATIO - (WEEKS LATER)
scenes.summary: Paul shows Venus and Richard footage of Jennifer Capriati dominating at the French Open and discusses her coach Rick Macci.

scenes.heading: EXT. CARBON MESA HIGH SCHOOL - DAY
scenes.summary: The Williams family arrives at Venus's first tournament where rich white tennis parents stare at them as the only black faces.

scenes.heading: EXT. CARBON MESA HIGH SCHOOL - JUNIOR TOURNAMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: The Williams family walks through the tournament courts as all eyes stare at them, with Richard reassuring them about the attention.

scenes.heading: EXT. CARBON MESA HIGH SCHOOL - TENNIS COURT - TOURNAMENT -
scenes.summary: Venus plays her first tournament match against Stacey, a tall blonde opponent with expensive equipment, while Richard nervously paces.
scenes.turningPointNote: Venus's first competitive tournament match - a crucial test of their training and preparation

scenes.heading: EXT. PALOS VERDES COUNTRY CLUB (VENUE #6) - DAY
scenes.summary: Venus competes in the championship match at Palos Verdes Country Club against a towering blonde opponent in front of 75 spectators.

scenes.heading: INT. RICHARD’S VW BUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Richard drives his family home after Venus's tournament win while the girls celebrate with her trophy in the back seat, but Richard remains visibly uneasy and upset despite the victory.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. RICHARD'S VW BUS/BODEGA - PARKING LOT -
scenes.summary: Richard gives the girls money to buy drinks at a bodega, then drives away leaving them stranded as punishment for bragging about tennis, until Oracene forces him to stop and return.

scenes.heading: INT. WILLIAMS’ HOME (COMPTON) - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Richard holds a family meeting where he shows the girls Disney's Cinderella and afterward quizzes them about the lessons they learned from the movie.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST COMPTON PARK - TENNIS COURTS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Richard practices with Venus and Serena in the rain at night, teaching them to hit wet balls before they bounce because they won't bounce properly on the slippery court.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST COMPTON PARK - A SHORT WHILE LATER
scenes.summary: After practice ends, TD and other gang members approach Richard and his daughters, but TD encourages Venus to keep bringing trophies home to Compton.

scenes.heading: EXT. WILLIAMS’ HOME (COMPTON) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The family arrives home to find a sheriff's vehicle parked in Richard's spot, causing Richard and Venus to wonder why police are at their house.

scenes.heading: INT. WILLIAMS' HOME (COMPTON) - THAT MOMENT
scenes.summary: Richard and his family enter their home to find two deputy sheriffs and a social worker searching their kitchen and questioning their parenting after receiving an anonymous complaint.

scenes.heading: EXT. WILLIAMS’ HOME (COMPTON) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: After the authorities leave, Oracene confronts neighbor Ms. Strickland at her door, knowing she made the call to social services about their family.

scenes.heading: EXT. CALIFORNIA UNIVERSITY - JUNIOR TENNIS OPEN - DAY
scenes.summary: At another tournament, Venus checks in for her match while Serena secretly approaches the registration table after the family leaves to watch Venus play.

scenes.heading: EXT. CALIFORNIA U. - JUNIOR OPEN - TENNIS COURT - LATER
scenes.summary: Venus dominates her match on court while Richard watches from the stands, until another father tells Richard his younger daughter also looks like a champion.

scenes.heading: EXT. CALIFORNIA U. - JUNIOR OPEN - COURT 2 - MOMENTS
scenes.summary: Richard rushes to another court where a crowd has gathered to watch Serena playing her own match, and Oracene stops him from pulling her off the court.

scenes.heading: EXT. CALIFORNIA UNIVERSITY - JUNIOR TENNIS OPEN - LATER
scenes.summary: Venus and Serena celebrate with ice cream, each holding trophies, and Venus offers to swap her gold trophy for Serena's silver one, which Serena eagerly accepts.

scenes.heading: INT. WILLIAMS’ HOUSE (COMPTON) - EVENING
scenes.summary: Venus reads aloud from a New York Times article about her undefeated record and recent tournament victory while the whole family celebrates around the dinner table.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVIERA COUNTRY CLUB - DAY
scenes.summary: At a charity tennis event, Nancy Reagan speaks to inner-city kids including Venus and Serena about saying no to drugs while photographers take pictures.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVIERA COUNTRY CLUB - DAY (LATER)
scenes.summary: Paul introduces Richard to two tennis agents, George MacArthur and Laird Stabler, who pitch their services to help take Venus to the next level while Richard watches his daughters play.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVIERA COUNTRY CLUB - POOLSIDE - DAY
scenes.summary: Richard finds Venus and Serena eating expensive food by the pool with wealthy club girls, and angrily confronts them about abandoning the food he packed for them.

scenes.heading: EXT. WILLIAMS’ HOUSE (COMPTON) - LATE AFTERNOON (SAME
scenes.summary: Richard tells Paul he's pulling Venus out of junior tournaments and refusing to sign with agents, shocking both Paul and Oracene with his sudden decision.
scenes.turningPointNote: Richard makes the pivotal decision to withdraw Venus from junior tennis entirely, fundamentally changing their trajectory despite everyone's objections.

scenes.heading: EXT. RICK MACCI TENNIS ACADEMY - NIGHT (ESTABLISHING)
scenes.summary: Rick Macci coaches teenage players in an energetic nighttime practice session at his Florida tennis academy, creating an atmosphere of intense training and competition.

scenes.heading: EXT. RICK MACCI TENNIS ACADEMY - PATIO - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rick Macci takes a phone call at his academy's patio while observing his players practice and telling one player to stop eating unhealthy food.

scenes.heading: EXT. WILLIAMS’ HOUSE (COMPTON) - DAY
scenes.summary: Rick Macci arrives by taxi at the Williams house where Richard and the entire family greet him on the porch in an organized formation.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST COMPTON PARK - DAY
scenes.summary: At the Compton courts, gang members confront Macci until Richard vouches for him, then Macci watches Venus and Serena practice on the deteriorated courts.

scenes.heading: INT. WILLIAMS’ HOUSE (COMPTON) - DAY
scenes.summary: Rick Macci presents his standard contract offer to the Williams family, promising housing, education, and training in exchange for 15% of future earnings, but Richard remains skeptical.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERT (ARIZONA) - DAY
scenes.summary: The Williams family travels cross-country in a new motor home, driving through Arizona and New Mexico as Richard sings to Oracene while the girls sleep.

scenes.heading: EXT. WEST PALM BEACH, FLORIDA (ESTABLISHING)
scenes.summary: An establishing shot shows the family arriving in West Palm Beach, Florida.

scenes.heading: EXT. RICK MACCI TENNIS ACADEMY (ESTABLISHING)
scenes.summary: The Williams family's motor home pulls through the gates of Rick Macci's Florida tennis academy.

scenes.heading: EXT. RICK MACCI ACADEMY - VARIOUS SHOTS - DAY
scenes.summary: Rick Macci warmly welcomes the Williams family to his academy and shows them around the impressive facilities, including his golf cart that he calls 'the comet.'

scenes.heading: EXT. THE PATIO AREA - MACCI’S ACADEMY - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Macci shows the family the patio dining area with unlimited food, then points out the premium courts #14 and #15 where Venus and Serena will train, distinguishing them from the lesser courts.

scenes.heading: EXT. RICK MACCI TENNIS ACADEMY - COURTS #14 AND #15 -
scenes.summary: Three years later, 14-year-old Venus and 13-year-old Serena have grown into powerful athletes with braces and new hairstyles, crushing serves and volleys under Macci's coaching.

scenes.heading: EXT. RICK MACCI TENNIS ACADEMY - COURTS 14 AND 15 - A FEW
scenes.summary: After a media interview, Venus returns to find Serena sparring with Macci, then notices Anna Kournikova practicing on center court for an upcoming junior tournament.

scenes.heading: EXT. RICK MACCI ACADEMY - PATH NEAR COURT 7 - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Richard leads Venus, Serena, and inner-city kids through Macci's academy while speaking to a newspaper reporter about not rushing the girls' education, as Macci watches with concern.

scenes.heading: EXT. RICK MACCI TENNIS ACADEMY - CAFE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Richard eats at the academy cafe when a breaking news report comes on TV about Jennifer Capriati being arrested for marijuana possession at a Florida motel.

scenes.heading: EXT. RICK MACCI TENNIS ACADEMY - COURTS 14 AND 15 - DAY
scenes.summary: Macci arrives for practice to find his assistants hitting balls alone because Richard canceled practice to take the girls to Disney World without telling him.

scenes.heading: EXT. WILLIAMS’ HOUSE (FLORIDA) - DAY
scenes.summary: Macci confronts Richard about missing practice for Disney World and learns that Richard has decided to pull Venus from an upcoming tournament, causing Venus to storm off upset.

scenes.heading: INT. WILLIAMS' HOME (FLORIDA) - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Richard and Oracene have a heated argument about his controlling decisions, with Oracene accusing him of thinking he did everything alone and Richard claiming she never believed in his plan.

scenes.heading: EXT. RICK MACCI TENNIS ACADEMY - TENNIS COURTS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Venus practices alone at night, furiously hitting balls until Richard arrives and begins feeding her balls like old times, leading to an intense father-daughter practice session.

scenes.heading: EXT. BANK OF THE WEST TOURNAMENT - DAY (VARIOUS SHOTS)
scenes.summary: An ESPN reporter introduces the Bank of the West tournament in Oakland where Venus will make her professional debut against top players including world number one Arantxa Sanchez Vicario.

scenes.heading: INT. TUNNELS -- OAKLAND COLOSSEUM - DAY (VARIOUS SHOTS)
scenes.summary: A WTA official leads Venus and her family through the arena tunnels as they pass by world number one Arantxa Sanchez Vicario being treated like royalty by tournament staff.

scenes.heading: INT. LOCKER ROOM - OAKLAND COLOSSEUM ARENA - DAY
scenes.summary: Venus enters a locker room filled with adult international players speaking different languages, who all stare skeptically at the teenage newcomer.

scenes.heading: EXT. PRACTICE COURTS - DAY (VARIOUS SHOTS)
scenes.summary: Venus practices on the courts wearing a Raiders hat while press and fans watch intently, as an ESPN reporter discusses questions about how she'll handle professional pressure.

scenes.heading: EXT. PRACTICE COURTS - OAKLAND ARENA - DAY
scenes.summary: Richard gives an on-camera interview expressing his fears that after nine years of preparation, they might discover they don't belong and should return to the ghetto.

scenes.heading: INT. MARRIOTT HOTEL (OAKLAND) - WILLIAMS' SUITE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In their hotel suite, Richard and Macci watch the ESPN report when a Nike representative arrives with a contract offer, presenting Venus with a substantial endorsement deal.

scenes.heading: EXT. SATELLITE COURT - OAKLAND COLOSSEUM ARENA - SAME
scenes.summary: Venus struggles in her professional debut match against Shaun Stafford on a packed satellite court, falling behind early while Richard stands with his back turned, unable to watch.
scenes.turningPointNote: Venus's first professional match represents the culmination of years of preparation and the ultimate test of Richard's unconventional training methods.

scenes.heading: EXT. SATELLITE COURT - OAKLAND COLOSSEUM ARENA - DAY
scenes.summary: Venus battles through early nerves with double faults and errors while her family and Nike representatives watch anxiously, as Richard paces nervously encouraging her.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY -- OAKLAND COLOSSEUM ARENA - DAY
scenes.summary: After winning her match, Venus gives a confident post-match interview to ESPN reporters, stating she wasn't surprised by her performance because she knows no one can stop her.

scenes.heading: INT. LOCKER ROOM - OAKLAND COLOSSEUM ARENA - DAY
scenes.summary: Venus enters the locker room and passes through a mix of congratulatory and jealous players while maintaining her composure.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - LOCKER ROOM - COLOSSEUM - THAT MOMENT
scenes.summary: Venus locks herself in a bathroom stall and finally lets out her joy and excitement in private celebration before quickly composing herself when someone knocks.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - OAKLAND COLOSSEUM ARENA - THAT MOMENT
scenes.summary: Richard finds Serena standing alone looking at the main stadium court and acknowledges that while this is Venus's moment, he understands it's difficult for Serena to watch.

scenes.heading: INT. MARRIOTT HOTEL (OAKLAND) - WILLIAMS’ SUITE - DAY
scenes.summary: Oracene works on Venus and Serena's hair while discussing Sojourner Truth and teaching them about being strong young black women who deserve to be seen and heard.

scenes.heading: EXT. OAKLAND COLOSSEUM ARENA - NIGHT (ESTABLISHING)
scenes.summary: A drone shot reveals the packed center court arena at night with an electric atmosphere as the announcer sets up Venus's second round match.

scenes.heading: INT. ANNOUNCERS BOOTH, OAKLAND COLOSSEUM ARENA - SAME
scenes.summary: TV announcers introduce the highly anticipated center court match between reigning champion Arantxa Sanchez Vicario and 14-year-old Venus Williams before a sold-out crowd.

scenes.heading: INT. COURT / HALLWAY - OAKLAND COLOSSEUM ARENA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Venus emerges from the tunnel wearing her iconic white beaded braids for the first time as she's introduced to a packed stadium for her professional debut match against Vicario.
scenes.turningPointNote: Venus's iconic debut moment - her transformation into the legendary player she'll become, marked by the famous beaded hairstyle

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - OAKLAND COLOSSEUM ARENA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Richard watches Vicario on TV exiting the court, then sees the real Vicario pass by him in the tunnel with little urgency as she heads to the locker room.

scenes.heading: EXT. OAKLAND COLOSSEUM ARENA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Venus waits alone on court as minutes tick by while Vicario stays in the locker room, with Macci explaining to Oracene that this is a deliberate 'icing' tactic to disrupt Venus's rhythm.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - OAKLAND COLOSSEUM ARENA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Richard watches Venus on TV killing time and grows increasingly angry as he counts the minutes, understanding exactly what Vicario is doing as a psychological tactic.

scenes.heading: EXT. OAKLAND COLOSSEUM ARENA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: After Vicario returns from her 10-minute delay, Venus appears tight and makes errors, hitting shots into the net while Vicario blasts winners, clearly affecting Venus's performance and the crowd's energy.

scenes.heading: INT. LOCKER ROOM - OAKLAND COLOSSEUM ARENA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the locker room after her loss, Venus cries like the 14-year-old she is, apologizing to Richard about the deals and respect, while he comforts her and praises her performance against the world's best player.

scenes.heading: EXT. OAKLAND COLOSSEUM ARENA - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Richard leads his family across the empty court as stadium lights shut off, and they climb the arena steps toward the exit doors.

scenes.heading: EXT. PLAYERS’ EXIT - OAKLAND COLOSSEUM ARENA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A massive crowd of fans, many young Black girls, waits outside chanting Venus and Serena's names, moving both sisters to tears as they sign autographs while Macci excitedly tells Richard that Nike and other sponsors are calling.
scenes.turningPointNote: Despite the loss, Venus has achieved something greater - inspiring a generation and proving her marketability, with sponsors now competing for her

structuralWarnings.text: 2 of 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
